name: Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – circular route
name_original: Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Valdeprado Del Río, located in Cantabria, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by mountains, dense forests, and serene rivers. The municipality features rolling hills and well-paved surfaces, providing varied routes for cyclists. Notable natural features include the Ebro River, its impressive canyons, and the Embalse del Ebro reservoir. This landscape provides both invigorating climbs and tranquil rides. Best road cycling routes around Valdeprado Del Río The most popular road cycling route is Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views around the Embalse del Ebro reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – circular route, a difficult 54.9 miles (88.4 km) path. It features significant elevation gain and passes through historical sites and dramatic canyon landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Villanueva de la Nía – San Martín de Valdelomar loop from Los Carabeos, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Valdeprado Del Río is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and dense forests like Monte Hijedo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Valdeprado Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valdeprado Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Valdeprado Del Río offers a diverse road cycling experience, characterized by a blend of mountains, dense forests, and serene river valleys. You'll find rolling hills and well-paved surfaces, providing both invigorating climbs and tranquil rides. The region is traversed by the Ebro River and its canyons, offering dramatic backdrops and varied topography.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Yes, Valdeprado Del Río offers routes for various ability levels. While many routes feature significant elevation, there are also easier options. The guide lists 9 easy road cycling routes, providing gentler terrain for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Cañones del Ebro&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the serene &lt;strong&gt;Monte Hijedo Forest&lt;/strong&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Alsa&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Embalse del Ebro&lt;/strong&gt; reservoirs. Historical landmarks include the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covalagua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a striking natural area a short distance away.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004565148/embalse-del-ebro-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another challenging circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005114272/real-santuario-de-montesclaros-el-canon-polientes-villanueva-de-las-rozas-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Cantabria, including Valdeprado Del Río, is generally considered a great cycling destination. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with the forests transforming with the seasons. Summer can also be good, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some climbs might be more challenging in warmer weather.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: The road cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents through river valleys and dense forests.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Absolutely. The dramatic landscapes of Valdeprado Del Río offer several excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcabado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Corbera&lt;/a&gt; are notable spots where you can pause and take in the spectacular views, often overlooking the Ebro Canyons or the surrounding mountains and forests.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, many routes, especially circular ones, often start from villages or towns like Reinosa or Los Carabeos, where public parking facilities are typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for local parking options.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Valdeprado Del Río area?
A: Yes, the region offers several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005114272/real-santuario-de-montesclaros-el-canon-polientes-villanueva-de-las-rozas-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 54.9-mile (88.4 km) path with significant elevation gain, perfect for a longer ride.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs near the cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: Valdeprado Del Río and its surrounding villages, such as Arcera, offer local establishments where you can find refreshments. While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route are not listed, towns and villages along or near the routes are good places to stop for a break and refuel.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: For families, it's best to look for the easier-rated routes, which tend to have less elevation gain and may follow quieter roads or paths. While dedicated family-specific bike paths are not highlighted, the region's commitment to sustainable cycling tourism suggests a growing infrastructure. Consider routes with less traffic and gentler gradients for a more enjoyable family experience.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes in Valdeprado Del Río?
A: The duration of routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004565148/embalse-del-ebro-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; (36.3 miles / 58.4 km) typically takes around 2 hours 35 minutes. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005114272/real-santuario-de-montesclaros-el-canon-polientes-villanueva-de-las-rozas-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Santuario de Montesclaros, El Cañon, Polientes, Villanueva de Las Rozas – circular route&lt;/a&gt; (54.9 miles / 88.4 km), can take over 4 hours.
tips: Although somewhat unknown, this 17th century Sanctuary is a jewel of Cantabria. A place with charm and great tranquility, completely secluded in the mountains and with spectacular views in any direction. They even have accommodation and the butterfly exhibition is worth seeing.
A small cave church, directly carved into the sandstone rock. It is very restored and with later additions. Outside the church there are two early medieval tombs carved into the rock.

The Tower of the Church of San Roque, popularly known as the Cathedral of the Fish, rises above the waters of the Ebro reservoir. With the construction of the reservoir, between 1921 and 1945, towns in the region, such as Medianedo, La Magdalena and Quintanilla, disappeared under its waters. A must visit for everyone visiting the area - the place is super nice and quiet.
